property.name: java.library.path
property.value: C:\Program Files\Eclipse Adoptium\jdk-11.0.28.6-hotspot\bin;C:\WINDOWS\Sun\Java\bin;C:\WINDOWS\system32;C:\WINDOWS;C:\Program Files\Common Files\Oracle\Java\javapath;C:\Program Files\Java\jdk-11\bin;C:\Program Files\Eclipse Adoptium\jdk-11.0.28.6-hotspot\bin;C:\WINDOWS\system32;C:\WINDOWS;C:\WINDOWS\System32\Wbem;C:\WINDOWS\System32\WindowsPowerShell\v1.0\;C:\WINDOWS\System32\OpenSSH\;C:\Program Files\Eclipse Adoptium\jdk-11.0.28\bin;C:\Users\DELL\AppData\Local\Microsoft\WindowsApps;C:\Users\DELL\AppData\Local\Programs\Microsoft VS Code\bin;C:\Program Files\Eclipse Adoptium\jdk-11.0.28\bin;C:\Program Files\Eclipse Adoptium\jdk-11.0.28.6-hotspot\\bin;C:\Program Files\dotnet\;C:\Program Files\Eclipse Adoptium\jdk-11.0.28.6-hotspot\bin;C:\Program Files\Common Files\Oracle\Java\javapath;C:\WINDOWS\system32;C:\WINDOWS;C:\WINDOWS\System32\Wbem;C:\WINDOWS\System32\WindowsPowerShell\v1.0\;C:\WINDOWS\System32\OpenSSH\;C:\Program Files\Eclipse Adoptium\jdk-11.0.28\bin;C:\Users\DELL\AppData\Local\Microsoft\WindowsApps;C:\Program Files\Eclipse Adoptium\jdk-11.0.28\bin;%JAVA_HOME%\bin;C:\Users\DELL\AppData\Local\GitHubDesktop\bin;C:\Users\DELL\AppData\Local\Programs\Microsoft VS Code\bin;C:\Exercism;.

testcase.name: canBuyHeartyWithCardIfFundsSufficient
testcase.failure: java.lang.AssertionError: The balance on the card should decrease by the price of one hearty meal. Check the code terminal = new PaymentTerminal(); card = new PaymentCard(10); terminal.eatHeartily(card); card.balance(); expected:<5.7> but was:<10.0> at org.junit.Assert.fail(Assert.java:88) at org.junit.Assert.failNotEquals(Assert.java:834) at org.junit.Assert.assertEquals(Assert.java:553) at CardPaymentsTest.canBuyHeartyWithCardIfFundsSufficient(CardPaymentsTest.java:276) at java.base/jdk.internal.reflect.NativeMethodAccessorImpl.invoke0(Native Method) at java.base/jdk.internal.reflect.NativeMethodAccessorImpl.invoke(NativeMethodAccessorImpl.java:62) at java.base/jdk.internal.reflect.DelegatingMethodAccessorImpl.invoke(DelegatingMethodAccessorImpl.java:43) at java.base/java.lang.reflect.Method.invoke(Method.java:566) at org.junit.runners.model.FrameworkMethod$1.runReflectiveCall(FrameworkMethod.java:50) at org.junit.internal.runners.model.ReflectiveCallable.run(ReflectiveCallable.java:12) at org.junit.runners.model.FrameworkMethod.invokeExplosively(FrameworkMethod.java:47) at org.junit.internal.runners.statements.InvokeMethod.evaluate(InvokeMethod.java:17) at org.junit.internal.runners.statements.RunBefores.evaluate(RunBefores.java:26) at org.junit.runners.ParentRunner.runLeaf(ParentRunner.java:325) at org.junit.runners.BlockJUnit4ClassRunner.runChild(BlockJUnit4ClassRunner.java:78) at org.junit.runners.BlockJUnit4ClassRunner.runChild(BlockJUnit4ClassRunner.java:57) at org.junit.runners.ParentRunner$3.run(ParentRunner.java:290) at org.junit.runners.ParentRunner$1.schedule(ParentRunner.java:71) at org.junit.runners.ParentRunner.runChildren(ParentRunner.java:288) at org.apache.maven.surefire.junit4.JUnit4Provider.invoke(JUnit4Provider.java:112) at java.base/jdk.internal.reflect.NativeMethodAccessorImpl.invoke0(Native Method) at java.base/jdk.internal.reflect.NativeMethodAccessorImpl.invoke(NativeMethodAccessorImpl.java:62) at org.apache.maven.surefire.booter.ForkedBooter.main(ForkedBooter.java:75)

testcase.name: addingMoneyIncreasesTheBalanceOnTheCard

failure.message: Does adding money work? Check the code: terminal = new PaymentTerminal(); card = new PaymentCard(10); terminal.addMoneyToCard(card, 10); card.balance() expected:<20.0> but was:<10.0>

testcase.failure: java.lang.AssertionError: Does adding money work? Check the code: terminal = new PaymentTerminal(); card = new PaymentCard(10); terminal.addMoneyToCard(card, 10); card.balance() expected:<20.0> but was:<10.0> at org.junit.Assert.fail(Assert.java:88) at org.junit.Assert.failNotEquals(Assert.java:834) at org.junit.Assert.assertEquals(Assert.java:553) at CardPaymentsTest.addingMoneyIncreasesTheBalanceOnTheCard(CardPaymentsTest.java:354) at testcase.name: successfulExactChangeHearty

failure.message: When buying a hearty meal using 4.3 euros in cash (calling terminal.eatHeartily(4.3)) the money in the payment terminal should increase by 4.3, i.e. the amount should be 1004.3. The current state is: money: 1000.0, number of sold affordable meals: 0, number of sold hearty meals: 0

testcase.failure: java.lang.AssertionError: When buying a hearty meal using 4.3 euros in cash (calling terminal.eatHeartily(4.3)) the money in the payment terminal should increase by 4.3, i.e. the amount should be 1004.3. The current state is: money: 1000.0, number of sold affordable meals: 0, number of sold hearty meals: 0 at org.junit.Assert.fail(Assert.java:88) at org.junit.Assert.assertTrue(Assert.java:41) at CardPaymentsTest.successfulExactChangeHearty(CardPaymentsTest.java:129) at java.base/jdk.internal.reflect.NativeMethodAccessorImpl.invoke0(Native Method) at java.base/jdk.internal.reflect.NativeMethodAccessorImpl.invoke(NativeMethodAccessorImpl.java:62) at testcase.name: successfulAffordable

failure.message: When buying an affordable meal using 4 euros in cash (calling terminal.eatAffordably(4) the money in the payment terminal should increase by 2.5 euros, i.e. the amount should be 1002.5. The current state is: money: 1000.0, number of sold affordable meals: 0, number of sold hearty meals: 0

testcase.failure: java.lang.AssertionError: When buying an affordable meal using 4 euros in cash (calling terminal.eatAffordably(4) the money in the payment terminal should increase by 2.5 euros, i.e. the amount should be 1002.5. The current state is: money: 1000.0, number of sold affordable meals: 0, number of sold hearty meals: 0 at org.junit.Assert.fail(Assert.java:88) at org.junit.Assert.assertTrue(Assert.java:41) at testcase.name: canBuyAffordableWithCardIfFundsBarelySufficient
testcase.failure: java.lang.AssertionError: The balance on the card should drop to zero if we buy an affordable meal when the balance is exactly the price of that meal. Check the code: terminal = new PaymentTerminal(); card = new PaymentCard(2.5); terminal.eatAffordably(card); card.balance(); expected:<0.0> but was:<2.5> at org.junit.Assert.fail(Assert.java:88) at org.junit.Assert.failNotEquals(Assert.java:834) at org.junit.Assert.assertEquals(Assert.java:553) at CardPaymentsTest.canBuyAffordableWithCardIfFundsBarelySufficient(CardPaymentsTest.java:223) at testcase.name: addingNegativeAmountDoesNotChangeCardBalance

failure.message: Adding a negative amount should neither change the balance on the card nor the money in the terminal! Check the code: terminal = new PaymentTerminal(); card = new PaymentCard(10); terminal.addMoneyToCard(card, -10); card.balance() expected:<10.0> but was:<-10.0>

testcase.failure: java.lang.AssertionError: Adding a negative amount should neither change the balance on the card nor the money in the terminal! Check the code: terminal = new PaymentTerminal(); card = new PaymentCard(10); terminal.addMoneyToCard(card, -10); card.balance() expected:<10.0> but was:<-10.0> at org.junit.Assert.fail(Assert.java:88) at org.junit.Assert.failNotEquals(Assert.java:834) at org.junit.Assert.assertEquals(Assert.java:553) at CardPaymentsTest.addingNegativeAmountDoesNotChangeCardBalance(CardPaymentsTest.java:369) at java.base/jdk.internal.reflect.NativeMethodAccessorImpl.invoke0(Native Method) at testcase.name: successfulExactChangeAffordable

failure.message: When buying an affordable meal using 2.5 euros in cash (calling terminal.eatAffordably(2.5)) the money in the payment terminal should increase by 2.5, i.e. the amount should be 1002.5. The current state is: money: 1000.0, number of sold affordable meals: 0, number of sold hearty meals: 0

testcase.name: canBuyAffordableWithCardIfFundsSufficient
testcase.failure: java.lang.AssertionError: The balance on the card should decrease by the price of one affordable meal. Check the code: terminal = new PaymentTerminal(); card = new PaymentCard(10); terminal.eatAffordably(card); card.balance(); expected:<7.5> but was:<10.0> at org.junit.Assert.fail(Assert.java:88) at org.junit.Assert.failNotEquals(Assert.java:834) at org.junit.Assert.assertEquals(Assert.java:553) at CardPaymentsTest.canBuyAffordableWithCardIfFundsSufficient(CardPaymentsTest.java:206) at java.base/jdk.internal.reflect.NativeMethodAccessorImpl.invoke0(Native Method) at java.base/jdk.internal.reflect.NativeMethodAccessorImpl.invoke(NativeMethodAccessorImpl.java:62) at testcase.name: successfulHearty

failure.message: When buying a hearty meal using 5 euros in cash (calling terminal.eatHeartily(5)) the money in the payment terminal should increase by 4.3 euros, i.e. the amount should be 1004.3. The current state is: money: 1000.0, number of sold affordable meals: 0, number of sold hearty meals: 0

testcase.failure: java.lang.AssertionError: When buying a hearty meal using 5 euros in cash (calling terminal.eatHeartily(5)) the money in the payment terminal should increase by 4.3 euros, i.e. the amount should be 1004.3. The current state is: money: 1000.0, number of sold affordable meals: 0, number of sold hearty meals: 0 at org.junit.Assert.fail(Assert.java:88) at org.junit.Assert.assertTrue(Assert.java:41) at CardPaymentsTest.successfulHearty(CardPaymentsTest.java:102) at java.base/jdk.internal.reflect.NativeMethodAccessorImpl.invoke0(Native Method) at java.base/jdk.internal.reflect.NativeMethodAccessorImpl.invoke(NativeMethodAccessorImpl.java:62) at testcase.name: canTakeMoneyFromCardIfBalanceIsEnough

failure.message: If the balance on the card is sufficient, then the takeMoney method should return true. Check the code: t = new PaymentCard(10); t.takeMoney(8); expected:<true> but was:<false>

testcase.failure: java.lang.AssertionError: If the balance on the card is sufficient, then the takeMoney method should return true. Check the code: t = new PaymentCard(10); t.takeMoney(8); expected:<true> but was:<false> at org.junit.Assert.fail(Assert.java:88) at org.junit.Assert.failNotEquals(Assert.java:834) at org.junit.Assert.assertEquals(Assert.java:118) at CardPaymentsTest.canTakeMoneyFromCardIfBalanceIsEnough(CardPaymentsTest.java:41) at testcase.name: multipleSold

failure.message: After executing the operations terminal.eatHeartily(5); terminal.eatAffordably(3); terminal.eatHeartily(5);terminal.eatHeartily(10); terminal.eatAffordably(4); the amount of money in the payment terminal should be 1017.9 euros. The current state is: money: 1000.0, number of sold affordable meals: 0, number of sold hearty meals: 0

testcase.failure: java.lang.AssertionError: After executing the operations terminal.eatHeartily(5); terminal.eatAffordably(3); terminal.eatHeartily(5);terminal.eatHeartily(10); terminal.eatAffordably(4); the amount of money in the payment terminal should be 1017.9 euros. The current state is: money: 1000.0, number of sold affordable meals: 0, number of sold hearty meals: 0 at org.junit.Assert.fail(Assert.java:88) at org.junit.Assert.assertTrue(Assert.java:41) at testcase.name: canBuyHeartyWithCardIfFundsBarelySufficient

failure.message: The balance on the card should drop to zero if we buy a hearty meal when the balance is exactly the price of that meal. Check the code terminal = new PaymentTerminal(); card = new PaymentCard(4.3); card.balance(); expected:<0.0> but was:<4.3>

testcase.failure: java.lang.AssertionError: The balance on the card should drop to zero if we buy a hearty meal when the balance is exactly the price of that meal. Check the code terminal = new PaymentTerminal(); card = new PaymentCard(4.3); card.balance(); expected:<0.0> but was:<4.3> at org.junit.Assert.fail(Assert.java:88) at org.junit.Assert.failNotEquals(Assert.java:834) at org.junit.Assert.assertEquals(Assert.java:553) at CardPaymentsTest.canBuyHeartyWithCardIfFundsBarelySufficient(CardPaymentsTest.java:295) at testcase.name: addingMoneyMultipleTimesWorks

failure.message: Does adding money work? check the balance on the card when you consecutively add the amounts 10 2 4 17 13 to the card expected:<56.0> but was:<13.0>

testcase.failure: java.lang.AssertionError: Does adding money work? check the balance on the card when you consecutively add the amounts 10 2 4 17 13 to the card expected:<56.0> but was:<13.0> at testcase.name: canEmptyCard

failure.message: One should be able to take all the money from the card. Check the code: t = new PaymentCard(10); t.takeMoney(10); expected:<0.0> but was:<10.0>

testcase.failure: java.lang.AssertionError: One should be able to take all the money from the card. Check the code: t = new PaymentCard(10); t.takeMoney(10); expected:<0.0> but was:<10.0> at org.junit.Assert.fail(Assert.java:88) at org.junit.Assert.failNotEquals(Assert.java:834) at
